title: Marine Le Pen’s comeback – European Council on Foreign Relations
gist: A Paris appeal court ruling has lifted Marine Le Pen's bar from office, letting her declare for France's 2027 presidential race, sideline protégé Jordan Bardella, and enter as the RN's favourite candidate, forcing centre and left rivals to recalibrate.

themes.theme: Le Pen's return and RN leadership

insights.claim: A Paris appeal court ruling rescinded Marine Le Pen's ban from holding public office, clearing her to run in France's 2027 presidential election.
insights.speaker: Célia Belin, Jeremy Cliffe, Mark Leonard
insights.so_what: Removes the base case that a more moderate RN figure leads into 2027, raising tail risk of a disruptive far-right presidency in a keystone EU state.
insights.locator: After a Paris appeal court ruling

insights.claim: Le Pen has reasserted leadership of the National Rally and sidelined Jordan Bardella, who was widely expected to lead the party into the 2027 election.
insights.speaker: Célia Belin, Jeremy Cliffe, Mark Leonard
insights.so_what: Signals a harder, less coalition-friendly RN candidacy than a Bardella ticket would have offered, shifting the risk profile for markets and Brussels.
insights.locator: After a Paris appeal court ruling

insights.claim: The National Rally enters the 2027 French presidential election as the favourite, prompting centre and left rivals to recalibrate their strategies.
insights.speaker: Célia Belin, Jeremy Cliffe, Mark Leonard
insights.so_what: A frontrunner far-right party raises French sovereign and EU-integration risk premia that investors should track into 2027.
insights.locator: Her announcement has led to rivals

themes.theme: Policy stakes of an RN presidency

insights.claim: An RN presidency under Le Pen could seek confrontation with Brussels and reshape France's approach to migration, the EU budget and European integration.
insights.speaker: Célia Belin, Jeremy Cliffe, Mark Leonard
insights.so_what: Confrontation with Brussels and EU-budget disruption from France would be a systemic risk to European cohesion and cross-border capital.
insights.locator: Would a Le Pen government seek confrontation

insights.claim: A Le Pen presidency is framed as potentially reshaping France's relations with Ukraine, the US and its European allies, with open questions over whether nationalist governments across Europe could actually cooperate.
insights.speaker: Célia Belin, Jeremy Cliffe, Mark Leonard
insights.so_what: France's stance on Ukraine support and transatlantic alignment is a geopolitical swing factor for defence and energy exposure.
insights.locator: And how would a Le Pen presidency impact

source_meta.author: Célia Belin, Jeremy Cliffe, Mark Leonard
